message.content:
[GAS TOWN] witness (rig: lesson_factory) <- self • 2026-02-22T04:35 • handoff

Check your hook and mail, then act on the hook if present:
1. `gt hook` - shows hooked work (if any)
2. `gt mail inbox` - check for messages
3. If work is hooked → execute it immediately
4. If nothing hooked → wait for instructions

content.thinking:
The startup hooks have already provided context. I have a patrol wisp hooked (ls-wisp-hz8). Following GUPP - I have work on my hook, so I execute immediately. No announcements, no waiting.

Let me check my hook and mail, then start executing.

content.text: Only one bead and it's not a patrol. Let me check what formulas are available and try creating the patrol properly.

content.content:
✓ Created wisp: 10 issues
  Root issue: ls-wisp-cvn
  Phase: vapor (ephemeral, not exported to JSONL)

Next steps:
  bd close ls-wisp-cvn.<step>       # Complete steps
  bd mol squash ls-wisp-cvn         # Condense to digest (promotes to persistent)
  bd mol burn ls-wisp-cvn           # Discard without creating digest

toolUseResult.stdout:
✓ Created wisp: 10 issues
  Root issue: ls-wisp-cvn
  Phase: vapor (ephemeral, not exported to JSONL)

Next steps:
  bd close ls-wisp-cvn.<step>       # Complete steps
  bd mol squash ls-wisp-cvn         # Condense to digest (promotes to persistent)
  bd mol burn ls-wisp-cvn           # Discard without creating digest

content.text: Good, patrol wisp created. Now let me hook it.

input.command: gt mol hook ls-wisp-cvn 2>&1 || gt hook set ls-wisp-cvn 2>&1

content.text:
The wisps are in vapor phase and not persisted. The beads infrastructure seems to have issues with the wisp lifecycle. Let me proceed with a manual patrol since the mol system isn't cooperating — the actual patrol work is what matters.

Witness, checking in. Running manual patrol.
No polecats in the rig. Let me check for any active beads or in-progress work.

content.text:
Rig is quiet — no polecats, no active work, just a standing crew bead and the rig itself. This is an idle town scenario.

Per the Idle Town Principle, there's nothing to patrol. No polecats to monitor, no lifecycle events to process, no stuck workers to nudge.

I'll use await-signal with backoff to sleep until there's activity, rather than spinning in a tight loop.
Patrol count is already at 15 — the handoff threshold. Per context management rules, I should hand off to a fresh session.

input.command: gt handoff -s "Witness patrol" -m "Completed 15 patrols, rig idle - no polecats, no active work. Handing off per cycle limit."
